title: MM-Math 开放题
expected_answer:
\textbf{Solution:} From the construction, we have that $MN$ bisects $BC$ perpendicularly,\\
$\therefore DB=DC$,\\
$\therefore$ the perimeter of $\triangle ACD = AC+AD+CD = AC+AD+BD = AC+AB = 5+12 = \boxed{17}$

title: MM-Math 开放题
rewritten_question_text: As shown in the figure, $AD$ is the median of $ \triangle ABC$, $CE$ is the median of $ \triangle ACD$. If the area of $ \triangle ABC$ is $12\text{cm}^2$, what is the area of $ \triangle CDE$?
expected_answer:
\textbf{Solution:} Since $AD$ is the median of side $BC$ of $\triangle ABC$, and the area of $\triangle ABD$ is $12\text{cm}^2$,\\
therefore, the area of $\triangle ADC$ is: $\frac{1}{2}\times 12=6\text{cm}^2$,\\
Since $CE$ is the median of side $AD$ of $\triangle ACD$,\\
therefore, the area of $\triangle CDE$ is: $\frac{1}{2}\times 6=\boxed{3}\text{cm}^2$

title: MM-Math 开放题
rewritten_question_text: As shown in the figure, $OP$ bisects $\angle AOB$, where point $E$ is on $OA$ with $OE = 4$, and the distance from point $P$ to $OB$ is $2$. What is the area of $\triangle POE$?
expected_answer:
\textbf{Solution:} Since OP bisects $\angle$AOB, and the distance from point P to OB is 2, \\
$\therefore$ the distance from point P to OA is also 2, \\
$\therefore$ $ {S}_{\triangle OEP}=\frac{1}{2}\times 4\times 2=\boxed{4}$

title: MM-Math 开放题
rewritten_question_text: As shown in the figure, $AB \parallel DE$, and points $B$, $C$, $D$ are on the same line. If $\angle BCE = 55^\circ$ and $\angle E = 25^\circ$, then what is the measure of $\angle B$?
expected_answer:
\textbf{Solution:} Since $\angle BCE=55^\circ$ and $\angle E=25^\circ$, we have $\angle BCE=\angle E+\angle D$. \\
Therefore, $\angle D=\angle BCE-\angle E=55^\circ-25^\circ=30^\circ$. \\
Thus, $AB\parallel DE$. \\
Consequently, $\angle B=\angle D$. \\
Hence, $\angle B=\boxed{30^\circ}$

title: MM-Math 开放题
rewritten_question_text: As shown in the figure, in $\triangle ABC$, D is a point on $BC$, $\angle 1=\angle 2$, $\angle 3=\angle 4$, $\angle BAC=108^\circ$. What is the measure of $\angle DAC$?
expected_answer:
\textbf{Solution:} Let $ \angle 1=\angle 2=x$,

$ \therefore \angle 3=\angle 4=\angle 1+\angle 2=2x$,

$ \because \angle DAC+\angle 3+\angle 4=180^\circ$,

$ \therefore \angle DAC=180^\circ−4x$,

$ \because \angle BAC=108^\circ$,

$ \therefore x+180^\circ−4x=108^\circ$,

$ \therefore x=24^\circ$,

$ \therefore \angle DAC=180^\circ−4\times 24^\circ=\boxed{84^\circ}$

title: MM-Math 开放题
rewritten_question_text: As illustrated, let $\triangle ABC$ be an equilateral triangle. Draw a perpendicular from point $D$ on side $AB$ to $BC$ at point $E$. Construct $EF \perp DE$, intersecting $AC$ at point $F$. Draw $FG \perp AC$, meeting $BC$ at point $G$, where $FG$ and $DE$ intersect at point $M$. If $DM = ME$ and $GE = 3$, what is the length of $AB$?
expected_answer:
\textbf{Solution:} As shown in the figure, draw MN $\perp$ BC at point N, \\
$\because \triangle ABC$ is an equilateral triangle, \\
$\therefore \angle B=\angle C=60^\circ$, \\
$\because DE\perp AB$, $EF\perp DE$, $FG\perp AC$, \\
$\therefore \angle BDE=90^\circ$, $\angle DEF=90^\circ$, $\angle GFC=90^\circ$, \\
$\therefore \angle DEB=180^\circ-90^\circ-60^\circ=30^\circ$, $\angle FGC=180^\circ-90^\circ-60^\circ=30^\circ$, \\
$\therefore \angle FEC=180^\circ-90^\circ-30^\circ=60^\circ$, $\angle DEB=\angle FGC$, \\
$\therefore GN=EN=\frac{1}{2}GE=\frac{3}{2}$, \\
In $Rt\triangle MNE$, $\angle MEN=30^\circ$, $EN=\frac{3}{2}$, \\
$\therefore \frac{NE}{ME}=\cos30^\circ=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$, \\
$\therefore ME=\frac{\frac{3}{2}}{\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}}=\sqrt{3}$, \\
$\because DM=ME$, \\
$\therefore DE=2ME=2\sqrt{3}$, \\
In $Rt\triangle BDE$, $\angle DEB=30^\circ$, \\
$\therefore BE=\frac{DE}{\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}}=\frac{2\sqrt{3}}{\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}}=4$, \\
$\because \angle C=60^\circ$, $\angle FEC=60^\circ$, \\
$\therefore \triangle EFC$ is an equilateral triangle, \\
$\therefore \angle EFC=60^\circ$, $EF=CE$, \\
$\therefore \angle GFE=90^\circ-60^\circ=30^\circ=\angle FGC$, \\
$\therefore EF=GE=3$, \\
$\therefore CE=3$, \\
$\therefore BC=BE+CE=4+3=7$, \\
$\therefore AB=BC=\boxed{7}$, \\

title: MM-Math 开放题
rewritten_question_text: As shown in the figure, in the isosceles right triangle $ABC$, $\angle BAC=90^\circ$, $D$ is the midpoint of $BC$, $E$ is a point on the side $AC$ (not coinciding with the endpoints), a line is drawn through point $E$ such that $EG \perp BC$ at point $G$, and $EH \perp AD$ at point $H$, a line is drawn through point $B$ such that $BF \parallel AC$ intersecting the extension of $EG$ at point $F$. If $AG=3$, what is the area of the shaded region?
expected_answer:
\textbf{Solution:} Let $DG=a$, $CG=b$, then $CD=a+b$, \\
since $\triangle ABC$ is an isosceles right triangle, $\angle BAC=90^\circ$, \\
thus $\angle ABC=\angle ACB=45^\circ$, $AB=AC$, \\
also since $D$ is the midpoint of $BC$, \\
thus $BD=AD=CD=a+b$, $BC=2BD=2(a+b)$, \\
since $EG\perp BC$, $EH\perp AD$, \\
thus quadrilateral DGEH is a rectangle, $\angle GEC=45^\circ$, \\
thus $DH=EG=CG=b$, \\
since $BF\parallel AC$, \\
thus $\angle FBG=\angle ACB=45^\circ$, \\
since $EF\perp BC$, \\
thus $\angle F=45^\circ$, \\
thus $GF=BG=BD+DG=a+b+a=2a+b$, \\
by the Pythagorean theorem, $AD^2+DG^2=AG^2$, \\
thus $(a+b)^2+a^2=9$, \\
which simplifies to $2a^2+2ab+b^2=9$, \\
According to the problem, ${S}_{\text{shaded}}={S}_{\triangle ABC}+{S}_{\triangle BGF}-{S}_{\text{rectangle} DGEH}$\\
$=\frac{1}{2}BC\cdot AD+\frac{1}{2}BG\cdot GF-DG\cdot DH$\\
$=BD\cdot AD+\frac{1}{2}BG^2-DG\cdot DH$\\
$=(a+b)^2+\frac{1}{2}(2a+b)^2-ab$\\
$=a^2+2ab+b^2+2a^2+ab+\frac{1}{2}b^2-ab$\\
$=\frac{3}{2}(2a^2+2ab+b^2)$\\
$=\frac{3}{2}\times 9$\\
$=\boxed{13.5}$

title: MM-Math 开放题
rewritten_question_text: As shown in the figure, it is known that $\triangle OCA \cong \triangle OBD$ and $\angle A=30^\circ$, $\angle AOC=80^\circ$. What is the measure of $\angle B$?
expected_answer:
\textbf{Solution:} Since $\triangle OCA \cong \triangle OBD$, $\angle A=30^\circ$, $\angle AOC=80^\circ$,
$\therefore \angle B=\angle C=180^\circ-\angle A-\angle AOC=180^\circ-30^\circ-80^\circ=\boxed{70^\circ}$

title: MM-Math 开放题
rewritten_question_text: As shown in the figure, in $\triangle ABC$, $\angle ABC=60^\circ$, $BC=16$, point D lies on the extension of BA, $CA=CD$, and $BD=10$. What is the length of $AD$?
expected_answer:
\textbf{Solution:} As shown in the figure, draw CE $\perp$ AD at point E,\\
$\therefore$ $\angle$BEC = $90^\circ$,\\
$\because$ $\angle$ABC = $60^\circ$,\\
$\therefore$ $\angle$BEC = $30^\circ$,\\
$\therefore$ BE = $\frac{1}{2}$BC = 8,\\
$\therefore$ DE = BD - BE = 10 - 8 = 2,\\
$\because$ CA = CD,\\
$\therefore$ AE = DE = 2,\\
$\therefore$ AD = \boxed{4}

title: MM-Math 开放题
rewritten_question_text: As shown in the figure, the straight line $CD$ is the perpendicular bisector of the line segment $AB$, and $P$ is a point on the line $CD$. Given that the length of segment $PA$ is $3\,\text{cm}$, what is the length of segment $PB$?
expected_answer:
\textbf{Solution}: Since line CD is the perpendicular bisector of segment AB, \\
it follows that PA=PB, \\
Since PA=3cm, \\
it follows that PB=\boxed{3cm}

title: MM-Math 开放题
rewritten_question_text: As shown in the figure, in $\triangle ABC$, $\angle ABC=90^\circ$, $\angle A=60^\circ$. Point $D$ is on side $AC$ with $\angle DBC=30^\circ$ and $AC=12\,\text{cm}$. What is the perimeter of $\triangle ABD$?
expected_answer:
\textbf{Solution:} Since $\triangle ABC\cong \triangle ADE$, and $\angle D=30^\circ$,\\
it follows that $\angle B=\angle D=30^\circ$,\\
Since $\angle C=70^\circ$,\\
then $\angle BAC=180^\circ-\angle B-\angle C=180^\circ-70^\circ-30^\circ=80^\circ$,\\
Since $\triangle ABC\cong \triangle ADE$,\\
it follows that $\angle EAD=\angle BAC=80^\circ$,\\
Also, given that $\angle CAD=35^\circ$,\\
then $\angle EAC=\angle EAD-\angle DAC=80^\circ-35^\circ=45^\circ$,\\
Thus, the answer is: $\boxed{B}$

title: MM-Math 开放题
rewritten_question_text: As shown in the figure, in $\triangle ABC$, it is known that points $D$, $E$, and $F$ are respectively the midpoints of $BC$, $AD$, and $EC$, and the area of $\triangle ABC$, ${S}_{\triangle ABC}=8$. What is the area $S$ of the shaded part?
expected_answer:
\textbf{Solution:}\\
Since point D is the midpoint of BC,\\
therefore, the area of $\triangle ABD$ equals the area of $\triangle ACD$ equals $\frac{1}{2}$ the area of $\triangle ABC$,\\
Since point E is the midpoint of AD,\\
therefore, the area of $\triangle BDE$ equals $\frac{1}{2}$ the area of $\triangle ABD$ equals $\frac{1}{4}$ the area of $\triangle ABC$, and the area of $\triangle CDE$ equals $\frac{1}{2}$ the area of $\triangle ACD$ equals $\frac{1}{4}$ the area of $\triangle ABC$,\\
therefore, the area of $\triangle BCE$ equals the area of $\triangle BDE$ plus the area of $\triangle CDE$, which equals $\frac{1}{2}$ the area of $\triangle ABC$,\\
Since point F is the midpoint of EC,\\
therefore, the area of $\triangle BEF$ equals $\frac{1}{2}$ the area of $\triangle BCE$ equals $\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{1}{2} \times 8 = \boxed{2}$

title: MM-Math 开放题
rewritten_question_text: As shown in the figure, in $\triangle ABC$, it is known that $BA=BC$, $\angle B=120^\circ$, and the perpendicular bisector $DE$ of $AB$ intersects $AC$ at point D. If $AC=6\,cm$, what is the length of $AD$?
expected_answer:
\textbf{Solution:} Connect $BD$,\\
since the perpendicular bisector $DE$ of $AB$ intersects $AC$ at point D,\\
it follows that $AD=BD$,\\
hence $\angle A=\angle ABD$,\\
since $BA=BC$, $\angle B=120^\circ$,\\
it follows $\angle A=\angle C=\frac{1}{2}\left(180^\circ-120^\circ\right)=30^\circ$,\\
thus $\angle ABD=30^\circ$,\\
hence $\angle CBD=90^\circ$,\\
thus $CD=2BD$,\\
hence $CD=2AD$,\\
thus $AC=AD+CD=AD+2AD=3AD$,\\
further, since $AC=6\text{cm}$,\\
it follows $AD=\boxed{2\text{cm}}$

title: MM-Math 开放题
rewritten_question_text: As shown in the figure, in $\triangle ABC$, $\angle C=90^\circ$, $AD$ bisects $\angle CAB$, if $AB=10$, $CD=3$, what is the area of $\triangle ABD$?
expected_answer:
\textbf{Solution:} As shown in the figure, draw $DE \perp AB$ at point E, \\
since $\angle C=90^\circ$, $AD$ bisects $\angle BAC$, \\
thus $DE=CD=3$, \\
therefore, the area of $\triangle ABD = \frac{1}{2}AB\cdot DE=\frac{1}{2}\times 10\times 3=\boxed{15}$

title: MM-Math 开放题
rewritten_question_text: As shown in the figure, $\triangle ABC$ is an equilateral triangle with side length 2, and the area of $\triangle ABC$ is $\sqrt{3}$. $D$ and $E$ are the midpoints of $BC$ and $AC$, respectively; $P$ is a moving point on $AD$. What is the minimum value of $PE+PC$?
expected_answer:
\textbf{Solution:} As shown in the figure, draw $BE$ intersecting $AD$ at point $P'$,\\
since $\triangle ABC$ is an equilateral triangle, with $AB=2$, and $AD$ being the altitude on side $BC$, and E being the midpoint of $AC$,\\
therefore, $AD$ and $BE$ are the perpendicular bisectors of sides $BC$ and $AC$ of the equilateral triangle $ABC$ respectively,\\
therefore, $P'B=P'C$,\\
therefore, $P'E+P'C=P'E+P'B=BE$,\\
based on the principle that the line segment between two points is the shortest,\\
when point P is at $P'$, $PE+PC$ has its minimum value, which is exactly the length of $BE$.\\
since the area of $\triangle ABC$ is equal to $\sqrt{3}$, and the side length is 2,\\
therefore, $\frac{1}{2}\times 2\times BE=\sqrt{3}$,\\
therefore, $BE=\sqrt{3}$,\\
thus, the minimum value of $PE+PC$ is $\boxed{\sqrt{3}}$

title: MM-Math 开放题
rewritten_question_text: As shown in the figure, in $\triangle ABC$, DE is the perpendicular bisector of the line segment AB. If the perimeter of $\triangle ABC$ is 18, and the length of the line segment AE is 4, then what is the perimeter of $\triangle BCD$?
expected_answer:
\textbf{Solution:} Since the perimeter of $\triangle ABC$ is $18$, \\
it follows that $AC+BC+AB=18$, \\
Since $DE$ is the perpendicular bisector of segment $AB$, and $AE=4$, \\
it follows that $AB=2AE=8$, and $DA=DB$, \\
thus, $AC+BC=10$, \\
therefore, the perimeter of $\triangle BCD$ = $BD+CD+BC=AD+CD+BC=AC+BC=\boxed{10}$

title: MM-Math 开放题
rewritten_question_text: As shown in the figure, fold $\triangle ABC$ along the segment $DE$ so that point $B$ falls on point $F$; if $AC \parallel DE$, $\angle A = 70^\circ$, $AB = AC$, then what is the degree measure of $\angle CEF$?
expected_answer:
\textbf{Solution}: We fold triangle $ABC$ along the line segment $DE$, such that point $B$ falls on point $F$,\\
thus, triangle $BDE \cong FDE$,\\
hence, $\angle DEB = \angle DEF$,\\
since $\angle A=70^\circ$, and $AB=AC$,\\
thus, $\angle B = \angle C = \frac{1}{2} \times (180^\circ - 70^\circ) = 55^\circ$,\\
since $AC \parallel DE$,\\
hence, $\angle DEB = \angle C = 55^\circ = \angle DEF$,\\
thus, $\angle FEC = 180^\circ - \angle DEB - \angle DEF = \boxed{70^\circ}$

title: MM-Math 开放题
rewritten_question_text: As shown in the figure, if $\angle A = 31^\circ$, then what is the value of $\angle A + \angle B + \angle C + \angle D + \angle E$?
expected_answer:
\textbf{Solution:} According to the given conditions,\\
\[ \because \angle AFG+\angle AGF=180^\circ-\angle A, \angle CFG+\angle AFG=180^\circ, \angle EGF+\angle AGF=180^\circ\]
\[ \therefore \angle CFG+\angle EGF=360^\circ-\left(\angle AFG+\angle AGF\right)=360^\circ-\left(180^\circ-\angle A\right)=180^\circ+\angle A\]
Also \[ \because \angle CFG=\angle B+\angle C, \angle EGF=\angle D+\angle E,\]
\[ \therefore \angle A+\angle B+\angle C+\angle D+\angle E\]
\[ =\angle A+\angle CFG+\angle EGF\]
\[ =180^\circ+2\angle A\]
\[ =180^\circ+2\times 31^\circ\]
\[ =\boxed{242^\circ}\]

title: MM-Math 开放题
rewritten_question_text: As shown in the figure, in \( \triangle ABC\), \(DE\) is the perpendicular bisector of side \(AC\). If \(BC=8\, \text{cm}\) and \(AB=10\, \text{cm}\), what is the perimeter of \( \triangle EBC\)?
expected_answer:
\textbf{Solution:} Given that $DE$ is the perpendicular bisector of side $AC$ in $\triangle ABC$,\\
thus $EA=EC$,\\
therefore $BE+EC=BE+EA=AB$,\\
given $BC=8$ cm and $AB=10$ cm,\\
thus the perimeter of $\triangle EBC$ is $BC+BE+EC=8+10=\boxed{18}$ cm

title: MM-Math 开放题
rewritten_question_text: As shown in the figure, $D$ is a point on $AB$, $DF$ intersects $AC$ at point $E$, $DE=FE$, $FC \parallel AB$, $AB=5$, $BD=1$. What is the length of $CF$?
expected_answer:
\textbf{Solution:} Given $FC\parallel AB$,\\
it follows that $\angle F = \angle ADE$, $\angle FCE = \angle A$,\\
In $\triangle CFE$ and $\triangle ADE$,\\
$\left\{ \begin{array}{l} \angle F = \angle ADE \\ \angle FCE = \angle A \\ FE = DE \end{array} \right.$,\\
thus $\triangle CFE \cong \triangle ADE$ (AAS),\\
thus $CF = AD$,\\
since $AB = 5$, $BD = 1$,\\
it follows that $AD = AB - BD = 5 - 1 = 4$,\\
thus $CF = \boxed{4}$,\\
hence the length of $CF$ is $4$
